# Arts & Culture

Topics in the **Arts & Culture** category of *the year 2012*.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Summary |
|-------|---------|
| [Adele's Album "21" — 2012 Dominance](adeles-21-album.md) | Adele swept all six of her categories at the 54th Grammy Awards on February 12, and *21* became the best-selling US album for a second consecutive year — an unmatched feat of the SoundScan era. |
| [The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey](the-hobbit-unexpected-journey.md) | Peter Jackson's first Middle-earth prequel, released December 14; grossed $1.017 billion worldwide and brought a controversial 48-frames-per-second high frame rate to mainstream cinema. |
| [Pro Evolution Soccer 2013](pro-evolution-soccer-2013.md) | Konami's football simulation, released in September; added all 20 Brazilian Série A clubs for the first time and earned an 82/100 Metacritic score in a critical comeback — the final PES title for Nintendo's home consoles. |
| [The Avengers (2012 Film)](the-avengers-2012-film.md) | Joss Whedon's Marvel ensemble, released May 4; the culmination of the MCU's Phase One grossed $1.52 billion to top 2012's box office and cemented the shared-universe model that reshaped blockbuster cinema. |
| [UEFA Euro 2012 Football Championship](euro-2012-football-championship.md) | The European Championship co-hosted by Poland and Ukraine, June 8–July 1; Spain's 4–0 win over Italy in the Kyiv final — the largest margin in the competition's final history — completed an unprecedented three straight major titles. |
| [Gangnam Style Viral Phenomenon](gangnam-style-viral-phenomenon.md) | PSY's satirical K-pop single, released July 15; on December 21 it became the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views, proving non-English music could achieve global virality through digital platforms alone. |
| [The Dark Knight Rises (2012 Film)](the-dark-knight-rises.md) | Christopher Nolan's Batman trilogy conclusion, released July 20; grossed $1.085 billion worldwide as 2012's third-highest earner, its release shadowed by the Aurora, Colorado theater shooting, and set the template for the auteur-driven blockbuster. |
| [Skyfall (2012 Film)](skyfall-2012-film.md) | Sam Mendes's James Bond film, premiered October 23 for the franchise's 50th anniversary; grossed $1.109 billion worldwide as 2012's second-highest earner and the highest-grossing Bond film to that date, while Adele's title theme became the first Bond song to win an Academy Award. |
| [The Newsroom HBO Series 2012 Premiere](the-newsroom-hbo-2012.md) | Aaron Sorkin's political drama, premiered June 24 on HBO; Jeff Daniels anchors a fictional cable-news network in an unapologetically polemical examination of journalism and corporate pressure, drawing 2.1 million viewers to its debut and running three seasons. |
| [Pussy Riot Punk Protest and Trial (2012)](pussy-riot-2012-trial.md) | The Russian feminist punk collective's February 21 "punk prayer" in Moscow's Cathedral of Christ the Saviour led to an August 17 verdict jailing three members for two years — an international cause célèbre over artistic freedom and political dissent under Putin. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
